## Summary

Adds a manuscript-only Storygate Studio FAQ/support page and links it from the Storygate Studio navigation dropdown.

## Why

The upgrade list calls out that Storygate Studio still needs support pages: manuscript-only FAQ, creator process explanation, industry-access explanation, and access/privacy trust copy. This PR adds that public support surface without expanding scope beyond current manuscript/publishing workflows.

## Changes

- Adds `/storygate-studio/faq`.
- Explains the creator process:
  - prepare manuscript package
  - clear readiness gate
  - choose what may be viewed
  - review requests and activity
- Explains the verified publishing professional process:
  - request verified access
  - use role-appropriate review
  - respect creator controls
- Adds access/privacy trust copy:
  - no public search
  - verified access
  - creator-approved visibility
  - logged activity
  - no guarantee of professional or commercial outcome
- Adds practical FAQ entries about qualification, supported projects, materials, public visibility, and limits.
- Adds `Storygate FAQ` to the Storygate Studio dropdown.

## Scope

Public support copy/page only. No backend, database, access-control logic, Storygate runtime, evaluation, scoring, Revise, TrustedPath, or Agent Readiness runtime changes.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Storygate has a dedicated FAQ/support page.
- FAQ remains manuscript-first and publishing-facing.
- FAQ does not promise unsupported non-manuscript workflows.
- FAQ clearly explains creator process, verified publishing access, controlled visibility, logged activity, and non-guarantee boundaries.
- Storygate dropdown links to the FAQ.

## Notes

The branch is small: one new page and one nav link. I could not run the app locally from the connector environment; CI/preview should validate render and build.
